key_findings: ACS (Asset Confidence Score) is backward-looking (confirmed presence) — orthogonal to KRIL (forward-looking, strategic value); high ACS + high KRIL = prime enrichment target; high KRIL + low ACS = likely wildcard/CDN, skip enrichment
Three-dimension model: DNS stability (0.35 weight), HTTP evidence (0.40 weight), service exposure (0.25 weight) — HTTP evidence weighted highest as strongest confirmed presence signal

Wildcard suppression via wildcard_flag=0 in DNS stability — ensures wildcard-detected subdomains score <25 regardless of HTTP response (CDN catch-all pages produce valid HTTP responses on wildcard entries)
Resolution consistency signal (same A records across last 3 scans) discriminates real assets from dynamic wildcard DNS — wildcard returns different IPs per query; real servers return consistent IPs
Partial ACS computed after DNS batch with http_evidence=0 and service_exposure=0; recomputed as HTTP and enrich data arrive; acs_computed_at timestamp distinguishes partial from full score
Enrichment gate: enrich_eligible = (kril_score >= 50) AND (acs_score >= 40) — requires both strategic value and confirmed presence before expensive nuclei/katana enrichment
All 14 signal component values stored as acs_signals{} on Subdomain node — analyst can inspect ACS reasoning without re-running scorer
Top-decile validation: after first full cycle, ACS top-10% should produce >=20% more findings/subdomain vs naive uniform distribution; validates score discrimination power

decisions.title: ACS as separate score from KRIL (not merged)
decisions.summary: ACS measures confirmed presence (backward-looking); KRIL measures scanning value (forward-looking); both required for enrichment gate
decisions.rationale: Merging ACS into KRIL would create a single score that tries to answer two different questions: 'is this asset real?' and 'is this asset worth scanning?'. A wildcard entry for a high-value domain would get KRIL boosted by false DNS resolution signals. Separate scores are independently queryable and independently configurable.

decisions.title: HTTP evidence weighted highest (0.40) over DNS stability (0.35) and service exposure (0.25)
decisions.summary: HTTP response is the strongest single evidence of a live, reachable asset; DNS resolution is necessary but insufficient; service exposure is valuable but often unavailable early
decisions.rationale: A subdomain that returns HTTP 200 with real content is almost certainly a live asset regardless of DNS instability or service scan gaps. DNS resolution is necessary but CDN edge nodes and wildcard domains also resolve. Service scan data is often unavailable until enrich phase.

decisions.title: Wildcard suppression via wildcard_flag=0 in DNS stability
decisions.summary: If wildcard_detected=true on subdomain, wildcard_flag signal is 0.0; DNS stability dimension capped below ACS threshold regardless of HTTP response
decisions.rationale: CDN catch-all pages return valid HTTP 200 responses for wildcard DNS entries. Without wildcard suppression, every wildcard subdomain in a high-value domain would score high ACS due to HTTP evidence. Wildcard_flag=0 reduces ACS to <25 range for detected wildcards.

decisions.title: Partial ACS computed immediately after DNS batch
decisions.summary: ACS computed with available signals at each pipeline stage; http_evidence=0 and service_exposure=0 until data arrives; recomputed as evidence accumulates
decisions.rationale: Waiting for full pipeline to complete before computing any ACS would delay enrichment gate decisions by the full pipeline cycle time. Partial ACS from DNS data alone allows early deprioritization of wildcard/CDN patterns before HTTP scan budget is spent.

decisions.title: Dual-gate enrichment threshold (KRIL >= 50 AND ACS >= 40)
decisions.summary: Both strategic value and confirmed presence required before nuclei/katana enrichment; thresholds hot-reloadable in score_config.json
decisions.rationale: KRIL-only gate sends enrichment to wildcard entries for high-value domains (expensive, noisy). ACS-only gate sends enrichment to low-value confirmed assets (uninformative). Dual gate targets the intersection: assets worth scanning AND confirmed to exist.

signals.name: resolution_confirmed
signals.formula: 1.0 if any A record present else 0.0

signals.name: resolution_consistency
signals.formula: 1.0 if same A records in last 3 scans, 0.5 if partial, 0.0 if all different

signals.name: status_code_quality
signals.formula: 1.0 if status in {200,301,302,401,403,404}, 0.5 if 5xx, 0.0 if no response

signals.name: title_present
signals.formula: 1.0 if page title present and non-empty else 0.0

signals.name: known_service_detected
signals.formula: 1.0 if any port maps to named service else 0.0

score_bands.label: moderate_confidence
score_bands.action: enrichment above KRIL threshold; normal finding weight

score_bands.label: weak_evidence
score_bands.action: enrichment only if KRIL also high; reduced finding weight

score_bands.label: low_confidence
score_bands.action: skip enrichment; low finding weight; reduced rescan priority

score_bands.label: very_low
score_bands.action: no enrichment; findings treated as likely false positive

computation_triggers.note: partial ACS; gates early wildcard suppression
full ACS; final score for reporting

calibration_validation.method: Labeled sample of 1000 subdomains across 5 ACS bands; compare predicted confidence vs actual confirmed_live rate per band

top_decile_validation.method: Sort by ACS DESC; compare findings/subdomain in top 10% vs bottom 90%

edge_case_handling.dns_stability_effect: resolution_confirmed=0 if no final A record

risk_model.title: CDN edge nodes score high ACS due to HTTP response
risk_model.mitigation: non_http_service_bonus and resolution_consistency penalize CDN patterns; CDN detection from httpx headers can zero http_evidence

risk_model.title: Calibration drift over time
risk_model.mitigation: Monthly calibration re-check against sampled labeled set; alert if calibration error exceeds 10%

risk_model.title: Enrichment gate excludes interesting low-ACS assets
risk_model.mitigation: acs_enrich_threshold hot-reloadable; analyst override via direct enrichment request

risk_model.title: Service scan data availability lag
risk_model.mitigation: Partial ACS computed without service dimension; final ACS after enrich; both stored with acs_computed_at

risk_model.title: Top-decile precision uplift <20% in early cycles
risk_model.mitigation: Validate after first full cycle; adjust dimension weights in score_config.json

risk_model.title: ACS score discontinuities on weight changes
risk_model.mitigation: acs_config_version stored with each score for audit provenance

recommended_actions.action: Add wildcard_detected flag check as first condition in DNS stability — prevents wildcard entries from scoring above ACS enrich threshold
Compute partial ACS after each DNS batch and store with acs_computed_at — enables early enrichment gate decisions before HTTP phase
Store all 14 signal component values as acs_signals{} on Subdomain node — analyst explainability requires this from day 1
Implement dual-gate enrichment: kril_score >= 50 AND acs_score >= 40 — reduces enrichment noise from high-KRIL wildcard entries
Add acs_enrich_threshold to score_config.json alongside kril_threshold — enables threshold tuning without code change
Run calibration validation after first full cycle: 1000 labeled subdomains across 5 ACS bands — validates score calibration before relying on it for reporting
Add ORS signal acs_age > 24h for active assets — triggers rescan for stale ACS scores
Monitor top-decile precision uplift after first full cycle; adjust dimension weights if uplift < 20%

assumptions: A1: DNS consistency signal requires at least 3 scan observations; partial score if fewer available
A2: wildcard_detected flag stored on Subdomain node from DNS validation phase
A3: Service scan produces open_port_count and service identifications stored on Port nodes
A4: HTTP evidence signals produced by httpx parser and stored on Subdomain node
A5: ACS computation async-pipelined at same architectural layer as KRIL scoring
A6: score_config.json hot-reload supports ACS weight fields
